title: centaurea glabroauriculata
intro: Centaurea glabroauriculata, also known as the sticky knapweed, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Centaurea glabroauriculata include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, which can help prevent infections. Centaurea glabroauriculata is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally or applied topically to the skin.
